feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
feed.subtitle: SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Commerce Automation with Non-SAP ERP Commodity codes
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Trying to understand the best possible ways to handle the commodity code (Taxonomy) in Ariba Invoice Management. We are implementing Commerce Automation and Ariba Invoice Management for a Non-SAP ERP backend, as part of this, we have a list of custom commodity codes that are used as Product groups in non-SAP ERP. I am aware of the 4 commodity code files (commodity export, Classification code map, ERP Commodity codes/Partition commodity code and CEME). But in our case, since the POs will be initiated from Non-SAP ERP System, when the invoices reach the Network, we expect the suppliers to submit invoices over the network. When suppliers submit the invoices over the network, it should flow to Ariba Invoice Management solution, and we should be able to capture the Commodity code details from the PO to Invoice Reconciliation document which will drive the accounting combination in Ariba Invoice Management as per the CEME (Commodity Export Map Export) combination.

From the above scenario, when the PO is created in Non-SAP ERP, we will not have the Commodity code (UNSPSC) captured anywhere on the PO Output to Network, but we may be able to send the ERP Commodity code / ERP Product group code. But when an invoice is submitted via network, will the Commodity code be determined automatically based on the ERP Product group code in Ariba Invoice Management?

Am looking for the best practices in this case on how we can leverage the Commodity code files in SAP Ariba Invoice Management while connecting with Non-SAP ERP System.

Please note that, since backend system is Non-SAP ERP, the Ariba Invoice Management solution is a Generic Variant provided to us and hence CIG is Out of scope, and we have BTP as middleware.

Any help or suggestions or how you have handled the above scenarios while enabling commerce Automation for PO Collaboration and Ariba Invoice Management for a Non-SAP ERP would be very much appreciated.

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Import Supplier Data and Account Information for Generic Ariba Variant
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are aware that the Supplier Data and the General ledger files can be automated from SAP ECC or S/4 HANA to SAP Ariba B&I through the Master data export program. But we have the backend System as non-SAP ERP and we have Ariba Invoice Management Solution (Not the B&I suite but only Ariba Invoice Management) as Generic Variant.

Can someone please confirm if we can import/Automate the Supplier Data and the Account (General Ledger) information from Non-SAP ERP System to Ariba Invoice Management provided we have SAP BTP as the middleware. Is this possible, was anyone able to perform the Batch Import of Supplier and Account information from Non-SAP ERP to Ariba Invoice Management/Ariba B&I?

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Commerce Automation PO and GR from Non SAP to Ariba Network and to Ariba Invoice Management Solution
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

Can someone please confirm if the below scenarios are possible? Our landscape is we use Non-SAP ERP for creating Purchase Orders and Goods Receipts and we have SAP BTP as middleware. We have Commerce Automation and Ariba Invoice Management solutions with us. 

1. Purchase Orders created, Updated and Cancelled from Non-SAP ERP System to Ariba Network and then to Ariba Invoice Management.

2. Goods Receipt created, updated or Reversed from Non-SAP ERP System to Ariba Network and then to Ariba Invoice Management.

Can someone please confirm if you have worked on the above scenarios in Ariba when dealing with a Non-SAP ERP System and how would you capture the PO updates and GR updates to send to Network and from there or directly from BTP, can we bring the PO and GR and their relevant updates to Ariba Invoice Management? Any help or guidance on this would be much appreciated?

Also, if you have any documentation or guidance on how to get the PO Copy from Network to Ariba Invoice Management solution, please guide.

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: SAP Watch List Screening and Ariba
entry.content:
Does anyone have any experience of implementing SAP Watch List Screening and SAP Ariba?

If so could you share how this works with guided buying.

 

Regards

entry.title: External Supplier review/approval in Service Entry Sheet (SES) workflow
entry.content:
Hello community members,
I have a query related to a specific business use case and need your input.

The buyer will create a PR request for lab and when lab submits their SES (Service Entry Sheet), we need an external Supplier to review/confirm/pre-approve the SES before it is sent to buyer for final approval.

How can this be configured in SES approval workflow ?

entry.title: why this (messaging-prod@smtp-c1.ariba.com) email address is picked when supplier sending mesaage to
entry.content: we are recieving emails from the above mail address, when suppliers are sending messages to the project team by using compose message option in sourcing event.

entry.title: Workday to Ariba integration for user data
entry.content: We have migrated our HCM system from SAP to Workday, and now need to keep our Ariba user population current with an automated integration from Workday HCM. Is this something that anyone has done in the past?

entry.title: Ariba LineItemsPOCloseImport encounters ERR_FR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS after migrating to async-xsjs
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

we are always encountering error ERR_FR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS when requesting to 
SAP Ariba LineItemsPOCloseImport api (https://s1.ariba.com/Buyer/soap/consulting-T/LineItemsPOCloseImport),
This feature is working before without the NodeJS upgrade and migration to async-xsjs even though the credentials are correct, we also try to misconfigure the credential for the authorization but this still returns the same error.

Please see the code below for your reference:

var dest = await $.net.http.readDestination("EXTERNAL_HTTP");
var client = new $.net.http.Client();
var req = new $.web.WebRequest($.net.http.POST, "");
req.headers.set("Content-Type", "application/soap+xml");
req.headers.set("Authorization", "Basic " + dest.username + ":" + dest.password);
req.setBody(soapenv);
await client.request(req, dest.host);
var response = await client.getResponse(); // -> this returns error after acquiring the response.

evidence of the error:

entry.title: Posting CxML Invocie from postman with PDF attachment to SAP Ariba Procurement
entry.content:
Hallo Team

Greeting!!

I am looking for an inputs how to post cxml invoice with attachment from postman to ariba procurement. I saw the document which is mentioned below
-------
<Attachment>

<URL>cid:1735239107.1592222922946@cxml.org</URL>

</Attachment>

 -----
How can I encode or create URL with cid URL>cid:1735239107.1592222922946@cxml.org</URL> to update the cxml payload


Thank you.

entry.title: Ariba import SupplierTaxRegistration via ITK
entry.content:
Hello,
I want to check anyone was able to successfully import SupplierTaxRegistration file in Ariba via ITK (import batch data) or is there any alternative way to import it via CPI.

entry.title: How to add tax code in catalog item upload?
entry.content:
Hi Developers team,

We face an error because a tax code was not created while creating the PR in the guided purchase interface. As of today, we've established the tax value on x amount as the current country setup, but after the PR is formed and integrated into our ERP system, it shows an error of PR sent with 0 value, and if we modify it to X amount, it says error format not supported.

To avoid future problems, I'm asking for your guidance on how to set up the tax code in catalog products uploaded into the system.

entry.title: SAP User Roles and SAP Ariba User Groups
entry.content:
What SAP User Roles are applicable for importing to SAP Ariba as User Groups? What would be an example of some SAP User Roles that would be imported to SAP Ariba as User Groups?

In the Managed Gateway for Spend&Network there is the ability to Maintain User Profiles for User Master Data.

According to the Learning Journey, Exploring the features of SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network, in the lesson on Maintaining User Profiles for User Master Data, it states that in the SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA on-premise, users must maintain all the SAP Roles that are assigned to SAP users that will be imported into the SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing solution.

I understand that when exporting user data from SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA (on premise), the information stored in the transaction code SU01 is transferred to SAP Ariba Procurement solutions.

I also understand that before extracting user master data from the SAP ECC or SAP S/4HANA on-premise, users must manually maintain all the SAP Roles as User Groups in the SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing solution. If this does not happen, the upload of user master data will fail. I believe that means it is important to ensure any SAP User Roles are also SAP Ariba User Groups.

I also understand that to establish User Groups in SAP Ariba Procurement solutions, it is necessary to utilize the import event Import User Group Mapping and define the Role Names from SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA (on premise).

I also understand that the Import User to Group Mapping Data (Consolidated File) data import task defines user-group mappings and is also used to make users responsible for specific purchasing units through their group assignments. It reads from the UserGroupConsolidated.csv file.

While I understand the process, I am attempting to understand the types of SAP User Roles that might be imported to SAP Ariba for use as User Groups. Are there any examples of these roles that would be imported? Is there a use case that could be provided as an example?

Thank you!

entry.title: Is it possible to combine multiple Purchase requisitions from different users into a single PO
entry.content:
Is it possible to combine multiple Purchase requisitions from different users into a single PO based on supplier , company code and delivery location  in SAP Ariba Buying and invoicing solution ? For example if there are multiple orders of a particular laptop from the same supplier and the delivery location is also same, we would like to combine the PRs into a single PO so that the delivery cost and carbon emission can be reduced. 

 

Regards,

Saurav Agarwal

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

entry.title: Master Data Retrieval API for Procurement
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to retrieve master data using master data retrieval API for procurement and the results were not returning correctly. 

1. Using the Search endpoint (/entities/{entityName})- there are entities that are returning results for example, entity User will show the results but for CostCenter, Business Unit it just return one value. See screenshot. The count shows there are 537 but only one was returned. 

2. The MetaData endpoint returns the fields for CostCenter for example but when I used it as $select field, the error occurs. I tried using Name or UniqueName but error occurs. For other fields like TimeUpdate, TimeCreated and other custom fields it worked just fine. 

Error: 

 "errCode": "400 BAD_REQUEST",
    "errMessage": "The following fields are not valid in $select: [Name]"
 

MetaData Result: 

 

Let me know if you encountered the same issue and how you resolved it. 

Thanks.

entry.title: You are not authorized to buy. Contact your administrator. - Guided Buying message while acting as
entry.content:
When trying to act as a user ,  if I add any catalog item to the cart in Guided buying , I do not see the checkout button. Instead a message is displayed "You are not authorized to buy. Contact your administrator." Is there any way , I can successfully create a Purchase requisition while acting as another user . The user does have there required groups ( like purchasing user etc. ).

 

Regards,

Saurav Agarwal

entry.title: Open Purchase Orders Migration
entry.content:
Hi Everyone - We’re working on an ECC to S/4 transformation project and our client has Ariba B&I realm connected to ECC today. The documents currently flow between the systems as explained below.

1) Create PR in Ariba -> Replicate PR to ECC -> Update ECC PR # in Ariba

2) Convert PR to PO in Ariba -> Replicate PO to ECC -> Update ECC PO # in Ariba

We need help to understand the best practices or approach we should follow to migrate the open PO’s to S/4 with this set up. For e.g do we need to convert open PRs and POs both as new documents in Ariba first and then replicate to S/4 or just convert the open POs from ECC to S/4 directly (which I think will cause issues in the integrations later if someone changes such POs after go live). Or what would be the right steps to migrate open POs in this case to make sure we don’t break anything.

Thank you all for the support.

entry.title: In GB, there is no validation for item category when creating ad hoc requestion
entry.content:
In GB, there is no validation for item category when creating ad hoc requestion using toggle for Goods/Services. Users are able to raise service requisition using Goods category and likewise. Can someone please help why CEME validation does not work in GB and what are the possible ways to resolve this.

SAP Ariba Procurement

entry.title: Wrong DUNS number synced to Procurement via sourcingnetworkid
entry.content:
Dear all,

we have the issue that every now and then the DUNS number set in Procurement gets overwritten from sourcing (the DUNS number in procuremt is required since the supplier validates it during POSR). I saw that we have a missmatch between transactionnetworkid &
sourcingnetworkid. Since sourcing was implemented before buying, sourcing uses a different SBN supplier than the one we have a TR with in Procurement. No attempt to align the IDs in Ariba worked yet.

Does maybe someone of you have suggestions on how this could be solved or the auto update be stoped?

Thank you a lot in andvance and best regards, Christoph

entry.title: I am looking for specific fields to use in Approval design to identify PR generated from quick quote
entry.content:
I am looking for specific fields to use in Approval design to identify PR generated from quick quote - zero touch. Can someone please help if it can handle via any existing field, or do we need any custom field for this? Please clarify. 
Requirement - Design approval flow to ignore a specific group when PR is created from Quick quotes - zero touch, kindly note PR is still treated as non-catalog after quick quote award.

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

entry.title: "Quantity could not be Changed from to" error when submitting the Ariba Purchase requisition.
entry.content: "Quantity could not be Changed from to" error when submitting the Ariba Purchase requisition. Its integrated system with SAP ECC
